lines.gurmukhi: ਨਟ ਮਹਲਾ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: natt mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Nat, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਦਾਸਨਿ ਦਾਸੁ ਕਹਤੁ ਹੈ ਹਮ ਦਾਸਨ ਕੇ ਪਨਿਹਾਰੇ ॥੮॥੧॥
lines.translit: naanak daasan daas kehat hai ham daasan ke panihaare |8|1|
lines.english: Nanak, the slave of Your slaves, says, I am the water-carrier of Your slaves. ||8||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਹਮ ਪਾਥਰ ਨਿਰਗੁਨੀਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: raam ham paathar niraguneeaare |
lines.english: O Lord, I am an unworthy stone.

lines.gurmukhi: ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕ੍ਰਿਪਾ ਕਰਿ ਗੁਰੂ ਮਿਲਾਏ ਹਮ ਪਾਹਨ ਸਬਦਿ ਗੁਰ ਤਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: kripaa kripaa kar guroo milaae ham paahan sabad gur taare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The Merciful Lord, in His Mercy, has led me to meet the Guru; through the Word of the Guru's Shabad, this stone is carried across. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਮੈ ਸੁਰਤਿ ਵਜੀ ਹੈ ਦਹ ਦਿਸਿ ਹਰਿ ਮੁਸਕੀ ਮੁਸਕ ਗੰਧਾਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: naamai surat vajee hai deh dis har musakee musak gandhaare |1|
lines.english: Through the Name, my awareness extends in the ten directions; the fragrance of the fragrant Lord permeates the air. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਨਾਮੁ ਦ੍ਰਿੜਾਏ ਅਤਿ ਮੀਠਾ ਮੈਲਾਗਰੁ ਮਲਗਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: satigur naam drirraae at meetthaa mailaagar malagaare |
lines.english: The True Guru has implanted within me the exceedingly sweet Naam, the Name of the Lord; it is like the most fragrant sandalwood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਗਾਵਤ ਗਾਵਤ ਹਰਿ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਏ ਗੁਨ ਗਾਵਤ ਗੁਰਿ ਨਿਸਤਾਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: gaavat gaavat har gun gaae gun gaavat gur nisataare |2|
lines.english: Singing, singing, I sing the Glorious Praises of the Lord; singing His Glorious Praises, the Guru saves me. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਤੇਰੀ ਨਿਰਗੁਣ ਕਥਾ ਕਥਾ ਹੈ ਮੀਠੀ ਗੁਰਿ ਨੀਕੇ ਬਚਨ ਸਮਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: teree niragun kathaa kathaa hai meetthee gur neeke bachan samaare |
lines.english: Your unlimited sermon is the most sweet sermon; I contemplate the most Sublime Word of the Guru.

lines.gurmukhi: ਬਿਬੇਕੁ ਗੁਰੂ ਗੁਰੂ ਸਮਦਰਸੀ ਤਿਸੁ ਮਿਲੀਐ ਸੰਕ ਉਤਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: bibek guroo guroo samadarasee tis mileeai sank utaare |
lines.english: The Guru is wise and clear; the Guru looks upon all alike. Meeting with Him, doubt and skepticism are removed.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਮਿਲਿਐ ਪਰਮ ਪਦੁ ਪਾਇਆ ਹਉ ਸਤਿਗੁਰ ਕੈ ਬਲਿਹਾਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: satigur miliai param pad paaeaa hau satigur kai balihaare |3|
lines.english: Meeting with the True Guru, I have obtained the supreme status. I am a sacrifice to the True Guru. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਾਖੰਡ ਪਾਖੰਡ ਕਰਿ ਕਰਿ ਭਰਮੇ ਲੋਭੁ ਪਾਖੰਡੁ ਜਗਿ ਬੁਰਿਆਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: paakhandd paakhandd kar kar bharame lobh paakhandd jag buriaare |
lines.english: Practicing hypocrisy and deception, people wander around in confusion. Greed and hypocrisy are evils in this world.

lines.gurmukhi: ਉਗਵੈ ਦਿਨਸੁ ਆਲੁ ਜਾਲੁ ਸਮੑਾਲੈ ਬਿਖੁ ਮਾਇਆ ਕੇ ਬਿਸਥਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: augavai dinas aal jaal samaalai bikh maaeaa ke bisathaare |
lines.english: At the break of day, they take care of their affairs, and the poisonous entanglements of Maya.

lines.gurmukhi: ਹਲਤਿ ਪਲਤਿ ਦੁਖਦਾਈ ਹੋਵਹਿ ਜਮਕਾਲੁ ਖੜਾ ਸਿਰਿ ਮਾਰੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: halat palat dukhadaaee hoveh jamakaal kharraa sir maare |4|
lines.english: In this world and the next, they are miserable; the Messenger of Death hovers over their heads, and strikes them down. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਆਈ ਰੈਨਿ ਭਇਆ ਸੁਪਨੰਤਰੁ ਬਿਖੁ ਸੁਪਨੈ ਭੀ ਦੁਖ ਸਾਰੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: aaee rain bheaa supanantar bikh supanai bhee dukh saare |5|
lines.english: When night falls, they enter the land of dreams, and even in dreams, they take care of their corruptions and pains. ||5||

lines.gurmukhi: ਕਲਰੁ ਖੇਤੁ ਲੈ ਕੂੜੁ ਜਮਾਇਆ ਸਭ ਕੂੜੈ ਕੇ ਖਲਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: kalar khet lai koorr jamaaeaa sabh koorrai ke khalavaare |
lines.english: Taking a barren field, they plant falsehood; they shall harvest only falsehood.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਕਤ ਨਰ ਸਭਿ ਭੂਖ ਭੁਖਾਨੇ ਦਰਿ ਠਾਢੇ ਜਮ ਜੰਦਾਰੇ ॥੬॥
lines.translit: saakat nar sabh bhookh bhukhaane dar tthaadte jam jandaare |6|
lines.english: The materialistic people shall all remain hungry; the brutal Messenger of Death stands waiting at their door. ||6||

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਿਤਨੇ ਕਰਜ ਕਰਜ ਕੇ ਮੰਗੀਏ ਕਰਿ ਸੇਵਕ ਪਗਿ ਲਗਿ ਵਾਰੇ ॥੭॥
lines.translit: jitane karaj karaj ke mangee kar sevak pag lag vaare |7|
lines.english: As much debt and as many creditors as there are, the Lord makes them into servants, who fall at his feet. ||7||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਨਮੁਖ ਕਰਜੁ ਚੜਿਆ ਬਿਖੁ ਭਾਰੀ ਉਤਰੈ ਸਬਦੁ ਵੀਚਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: manamukh karaj charriaa bikh bhaaree utarai sabad veechaare |
lines.english: The self-willed manmukh has accumulated a tremendous load of debt in sin; only by contemplating the Word of the Shabad, can this debt be paid off.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਗੰਨਾਥ ਸਭਿ ਜੰਤ੍ਰ ਉਪਾਏ ਨਕਿ ਖੀਨੀ ਸਭ ਨਥਹਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jaganaath sabh jantr upaae nak kheenee sabh nathahaare |
lines.english: All the beings which the Lord of the Universe created - He puts the rings through their noses, and leads them all along.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਟ ਮਹਲਾ ੪ ॥
lines.translit: natt mahalaa 4 |
lines.english: Nat, Fourth Mehl:

lines.gurmukhi: ਰਾਮ ਹਰਿ ਅੰਮ੍ਰਿਤ ਸਰਿ ਨਾਵਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: raam har amrit sar naavaare |
lines.english: The Lord has bathed me in the pool of Ambrosial Nectar.

lines.gurmukhi: ਨਾਨਕ ਪ੍ਰਭੁ ਖਿੰਚੈ ਤਿਵ ਚਲੀਐ ਜਿਉ ਭਾਵੈ ਰਾਮ ਪਿਆਰੇ ॥੮॥੨॥
lines.translit: naanak prabh khinchai tiv chaleeai jiau bhaavai raam piaare |8|2|
lines.english: O Nanak, as God drives us on, so do we follow; it is all the Will of the Beloved Lord. ||8||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸੰਗਤਿ ਕਾ ਗੁਨੁ ਬਹੁਤੁ ਅਧਿਕਾਈ ਪੜਿ ਸੂਆ ਗਨਕ ਉਧਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: sangat kaa gun bahut adhikaaee parr sooaa ganak udhaare |
lines.english: The virtues of the Sangat, the Holy Congregation, are so very great. Even the prostitute was saved, by teaching the parrot to speak the Lord's Name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਿਗੁਰਿ ਗਿਆਨੁ ਮਜਨੁ ਹੈ ਨੀਕੋ ਮਿਲਿ ਕਲਮਲ ਪਾਪ ਉਤਾਰੇ ॥੧॥ ਰਹਾਉ ॥
lines.translit: satigur giaan majan hai neeko mil kalamal paap utaare |1| rahaau |
lines.english: The spiritual wisdom of the True Guru is the most excellent cleansing bath; bathing in it, all the filthy sins are washed away. ||1||Pause||

lines.gurmukhi: ਪਰਸਨ ਪਰਸ ਭਏ ਕੁਬਿਜਾ ਕਉ ਲੈ ਬੈਕੁੰਠਿ ਸਿਧਾਰੇ ॥੧॥
lines.translit: parasan paras bhe kubijaa kau lai baikuntth sidhaare |1|
lines.english: Krishna was pleased, and so he touched the hunch-back Kubija, and she was transported to the heavens. ||1||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮੇਰੇ ਠਾਕੁਰ ਕੈ ਮਨਿ ਭਾਇ ਭਾਵਨੀ ਜਮਕੰਕਰ ਮਾਰਿ ਬਿਦਾਰੇ ॥੨॥
lines.translit: mere tthaakur kai man bhaae bhaavanee jamakankar maar bidaare |2|
lines.english: His loving devotion pleased my Lord and Master, who struck down and drove off the Messengers of Death. ||2||

lines.gurmukhi: ਅਜਾਮਲ ਪ੍ਰੀਤਿ ਪੁਤ੍ਰ ਪ੍ਰਤਿ ਕੀਨੀ ਕਰਿ ਨਾਰਾਇਣ ਬੋਲਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: ajaamal preet putr prat keenee kar naaraaein bolaare |
lines.english: Ajaamal loved his son Naaraayan, and called out his name.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਤਸੰਗਤਿ ਮਿਲੈ ਤ ਦਿੜਤਾ ਆਵੈ ਹਰਿ ਰਾਮ ਨਾਮਿ ਨਿਸਤਾਰੇ ॥੩॥
lines.translit: satasangat milai ta dirrataa aavai har raam naam nisataare |3|
lines.english: But when he joins the Sat Sangat, the True Congregation, he is confirmed in his faith, and he is saved by the Name of the Lord. ||3||

lines.gurmukhi: ਮਾਨੁਖੁ ਕਥੈ ਕਥਿ ਲੋਕ ਸੁਨਾਵੈ ਜੋ ਬੋਲੈ ਸੋ ਨ ਬੀਚਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: maanukh kathai kath lok sunaavai jo bolai so na beechaare |
lines.english: The mortal speaks and by speaking, makes the people listen; but he does not reflect upon what he himself says.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਲਗੁ ਜੀਉ ਪਿੰਡੁ ਹੈ ਸਾਬਤੁ ਤਬ ਲਗਿ ਕਿਛੁ ਨ ਸਮਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: jab lag jeeo pindd hai saabat tab lag kichh na samaare |
lines.english: As long as his soul and body are healthy and strong, he does not remember the Lord at all.

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਕਤ ਸਿਉ ਮਨ ਮੇਲੁ ਨ ਕਰੀਅਹੁ ਜਿਨਿ ਹਰਿ ਹਰਿ ਨਾਮੁ ਬਿਸਾਰੇ ॥
lines.translit: saakat siau man mel na kareeahu jin har har naam bisaare |
lines.english: O mind, do not join with the faithless cynic, who has forgotten the Name of the Lord, Har, Har.

lines.gurmukhi: ਜਬ ਘਰ ਮੰਦਰਿ ਆਗਿ ਲਗਾਨੀ ਕਢਿ ਕੂਪੁ ਕਢੈ ਪਨਿਹਾਰੇ ॥੪॥
lines.translit: jab ghar mandar aag lagaanee kadt koop kadtai panihaare |4|
lines.english: But when his home and mansion catch fire, then, he wants to dig the well to draw water. ||4||

lines.gurmukhi: ਸਾਕਤ ਬਚਨ ਬਿਛੂਆ ਜਿਉ ਡਸੀਐ ਤਜਿ ਸਾਕਤ ਪਰੈ ਪਰਾਰੇ ॥੫॥
lines.translit: saakat bachan bichhooaa jiau ddaseeai taj saakat parai paraare |5|
lines.english: The word of the faithless cynic stings like a scorpion; leave the faithless cynic far, far behind. ||5||
